name: Repost-with-agent
description: Skill-only OpenClaw/current-harness plugin for agent-driven, browser-based reposting (v4.5.7 — no CLI, no MCP server, no shell registration script). The running agent (OpenClaw via its built-in browser, Claude Code via chrome-devtools-mcp, or another explicit browser adapter) reads pair configs from ~/.repost-with-agent/pairs.json and uses its native tools to scrape sources, dedupe (Layer 1 string-match + Layer 2 agent semantic check), apply custom user skip rules, expand URLs without changing source wording, post via the user's logged-in browser, append history, manage source-item fanout manifests for scheduled backfills, and notify the user via the configured current-harness message route. NON-NEGOTIABLE RULES: public destination text must preserve original source wording exactly; if exact cleaned text will not fit, skip/block rather than compact, truncate, paraphrase, summarize, fix grammar, or otherwise reword. Successful source items MUST notify the user via configured delivery; source fanouts use one aggregate message with all destination outcomes. Silent publishes are a bug.
